$comment: Machine-readable inventory of every major README/status claim, with its supporting evidence and honest validation status. validation_status values: verified_local (a command/test on this machine proves it), measured (a reproducible evaluation artifact backs the number), implemented_untested_externally (code exists, no external run), pending (not yet done), superseded (an earlier claim retired by the hardening phase).

claims.claim: Synthetic ERP generator produces 22 datasets across smoke/demo/full profiles; counts smoke 13,882 / demo 247,881 / full 1,699,010.
claims.supporting:
evaluation/performance/profile_counts.json; data_generator/; `python -m data_generator.cli generate --profile

`
claims.known_limitation: Full profile: only generation was run and measured; downstream stages (ingest/dbt/rules/ER) ran at smoke and demo scale only.

claims.claim: 25 controlled defect types injected with isolated ground truth.
claims.supporting: data_generator/injectors/engine.py (ISSUE_TYPES, 25 entries); tests/unit/test_injection.py
claims.known_limitation: Synthetic perturbations; do not model all real-world defect variety.

claims.claim: Auditable, idempotent ingestion into DuckDB with row/file hashes and rejected-record handling.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/ingestion/loader.py; tests/integration/test_ingestion.py
claims.known_limitation: Per-row hashing is Python-side (pandas apply); not vectorized for full scale.

claims.claim: dbt builds staging, core star schema, and 7 marts on the local DuckDB target.
claims.supporting: dbt_supply_chain/; `python scripts/run_local_pipeline.py` (dbt build PASS report)
claims.known_limitation: Local DuckDB target. H4 added tests/end_to_end/test_dbt_pipeline.py which invokes the real dbt project (all 11 core models + 7 marts build and populate) and fails on any dbt breakage; a fixture-drift guard keeps TRANSFORM_SQL in sync with the dbt models.

claims.claim: 49-rule data-quality engine with evidence and scoring.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/quality/registry.py (49 rules); tests/data_quality/test_rule_engine.py

claims.claim: Against a validated clean baseline, SQL-detectable defect types (20) show recall 0.985 (194/197) and precision >= 0.933 (conservative lower bound); per-type recall and per-difficulty are reported, with all 25 injected types attributed to a detecting subsystem.
claims.supporting: evaluation/data_quality/detection_smoke.json; evaluation/data_quality/clean_baseline_smoke.json; scripts/evaluate_detection.py; scripts/validate_clean_baseline.py; tests/data_quality/test_clean_baseline.py
claims.known_limitation: Precision is computed by baseline-diff and treats unlinkable collateral (the same injected defect surfacing on a related record) as a false positive, so 0.933 is a conservative lower bound. obsolete_component_in_active_bom recall is 0.667 by construction (the injector does not guarantee an ACTIVE parent, so some injected records are not real violations). 3 duplicate types are evaluated by entity resolution (ml_eval.json); 2 types (conflicting_mpn, conflicting_part_descriptions) have no single-record SQL rule and are documented as unevaluated. Smoke profile.

claims.claim: Weighted entity-resolution baseline: recommend band P=1.00/R=0.57, review band P=1.00/R=0.86.
claims.supporting: evaluation/entity_resolution/baseline_smoke.json; scripts/evaluate_entity_resolution.py
claims.known_limitation: Smoke-scale, small positive population; baseline evaluates over all labeled pairs (no train/test split needed as it is unsupervised).

claims.claim: ML ER, leakage-safe: logistic regression P 0.962±0.010 / R 0.804±0.178 / F1 0.867±0.113; gradient boosting high-variance (R 0.471±0.375) and not recommended at this scale. Candidate-generation recall 0.95 over 409 labeled pairs.
claims.supporting: evaluation/entity_resolution/ml_eval.json; scripts/train_entity_resolution.py; tests/unit/test_ml_entity_resolution.py (entity-disjoint + connected-component + multi-seed tests); docs/model-card.md
claims.known_limitation: Replaces the retired P=1.00/R=1.00 claim (that split was not provably entity-disjoint and rested on ~6 positives). Folds are now connected-component entity-disjoint with a runtime part-disjointness assertion; metrics are mean ± std over 5 split seeds. Per-fold test positives still range ~20-140, hence the wide recall band. Synthetic duplicates only.

claims.claim: Field-level golden-record survivorship with full lineage; reversible; no warehouse mutation.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/golden_record/survivorship.py; tests/unit/test_golden_record.py
claims.known_limitation: No large field-level survivorship accuracy report against ground truth yet.

claims.claim: BOM graph intelligence: cycles, orphans, reverse dependencies, criticality, supplier concentration.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/bom_graph/graph.py; tests/unit/test_bom_graph.py (16 tests, all required shapes)

claims.claim: Quality Impact Twin computes blast radius and simulates counterfactuals without mutating baseline data.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/impact_twin/; tests/unit/test_impact_twin.py (baseline-immutability assertion)
claims.known_limitation: Exposure figures are estimates; no MRP/costing re-run.

claims.claim: Supplier-document extraction with evidence/confidence and prompt-injection controls; extraction unaffected by embedded instructions.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/document_intelligence/; tests/integration/test_document_intelligence.py
claims.known_limitation: Injection detection is pattern-based; novel phrasings may evade the list (flagged content is routed to review, never followed).

claims.claim: Governed AI remediation engine with schema validation, grounding, abstention, and audit; the mock provider is tested.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/ai/; tests/unit/test_ai_remediation.py (10 tests, incl. no-approve-action and grounding-rejection)
claims.known_limitation: The deterministic mock is the default and the only provider exercised by automated tests. H6 added AnthropicAIProvider (real SDK, fake-client tested) and scripts/validate_real_ai_provider.py; a real LLM call is pending an API key (the script skips cleanly without one).

claims.claim: Configurable real AI provider (Anthropic Claude) with schema-constrained output, grounding validation, retry/timeout, refusal handling, and token/latency audit.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/ai/providers.py (AnthropicAIProvider); scripts/validate_real_ai_provider.py; tests/unit/test_anthropic_provider.py (fake-client + skipped-without-key integration test)
claims.known_limitation: NEVER called against the real Anthropic API in this environment. Logic is verified only with a fake client. Run scripts/validate_real_ai_provider.py with a key to produce evaluation/ai/real_provider_validation.json and flip this to measured. Marked validated only after a successful run.

claims.claim: Snowflake target: warehouse adapter (SnowflakeWarehouse), modernized Cortex AI_COMPLETE provider, provisioning scripts with scoped AI grants, and a deploy/validate script. Fake-connection tested.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/warehouse/snowflake.py; src/bom_guardian/warehouse/base.py; src/bom_guardian/ai/providers.py (AI_COMPLETE); scripts/deploy_snowflake.py; warehouse/snowflake/**; tests/unit/test_snowflake_backend.py (12 tests)
claims.known_limitation: NEVER executed against a live Snowflake account. The adapter and AI_COMPLETE provider are verified only with a fake connection; write_pandas, real AI_COMPLETE output, and the provisioning scripts have not run against Snowflake. Status: implemented locally; external Snowflake execution pending.

claims.claim: FastAPI service exposes 29 versioned /api/v1 operations with pagination, structured errors, correlation IDs, OpenAPI, and role-based authorization.
claims.supporting: api/app/; api/tests/; docs/api-guide.md; count verified against app.openapi() (29 GET/POST operations)
claims.known_limitation: Authorization IS enforced (see role-based-authorization), but authentication is demonstration-grade (static demo bearer tokens), not SSO/OIDC. SQL is built with f-strings using a _safe() escaping helper plus allowlisted sort columns rather than bound parameters — documented in docs/security-model.md.

claims.claim: React remediation workbench (8 surfaces) renders live API data with loading/empty/error states.
claims.supporting: frontend/src/; frontend/src/**/*.test.tsx (5 vitest tests); `npm run typecheck && npm run build`
claims.known_limitation: Browser rendering was exercised via the accessibility tree, not pixel screenshots; automated screenshot capture is pending (H9).

claims.claim: Read-only Data Steward Copilot answers with citations, refuses mutations, over allowlisted parameterized queries.
claims.supporting: src/bom_guardian/copilot/; tests for copilot (read-only guarantee check)
claims.known_limitation: Deterministic keyword classification, not an LLM; unusual phrasings fall to the unsupported path.

claims.claim: Power BI package: 7 marts, semantic-model spec, 30+ DAX measures, theme, page specs, CSV fallback.
claims.supporting: powerbi/; scripts/export_powerbi_data.py; dbt marts
claims.known_limitation: No .pbix built or visually validated; DAX authored but never executed in a live model.

claims.claim: CI (GitHub Actions) runs Python 3.12/3.13 (lint, format, mypy, full pytest incl. real dbt E2E), frontend (lint/typecheck/test/build), dbt smoke pipeline, docs-link check, and secret scanning.
claims.supporting: .github/workflows/ci.yml; verified run https://github.com/Darshita-dp/AI-Powered-Supply-Chain-Data-Quality-and-BOM-Remediation-Platform/actions/runs/29671853670 on commit c4c8bbc (conclusion: success)
claims.known_limitation: Confirmed green on GitHub Actions for commits 13fa952 and c4c8bbc. Prior to H8 every run failed: the python job installed .[dev,api,ml] but test collection needs the docs-ai extra (reportlab/pypdf), aborting pytest with exit code 2. dependency-review is intentionally skipped on push (pull_request only). The optional snowflake-validation workflow is manual and still never executed (no credentials).

claims.claim: 182 Python (1 skipped) + 5 frontend automated tests, all passing; 93% line coverage.
claims.supporting: `pytest` (182 passed, 1 skipped, coverage 93% — measured in a clean CI-equivalent venv); `cd frontend && npm test -- --run` (5 passed); also green on GitHub Actions run 29671853670 for Python 3.12 and 3.13
claims.known_limitation: The single skip is the Anthropic integration test, which requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (reported explicitly via `pytest -rs`). Two E2E tests: a fast service-level one (TRANSFORM_SQL, with a drift guard) and a true dbt-pipeline one that runs the real dbt build.

claims.claim: Real application screenshots of all 8 UI surfaces, captured from the running app.
claims.supporting: scripts/capture_screenshots.py (real pipeline + FastAPI + Vite + Playwright); docs/screenshots/*.png + manifest.json; tests/unit/test_screenshot_capture.py (8 harness tests)
claims.known_limitation: Captured on a Windows dev machine against the smoke profile; values are synthetic and vary by profile/seed. The capture is not run in CI (it needs a browser download plus npm install), so the committed PNGs are refreshed manually via `make screenshots`. The script asserts live API values appear in the DOM before saving, and fails rather than emitting placeholders.

claims.claim: Role-based API authorization: analyst/steward/admin ladder; approve/reject/request-evidence require steward+; the recorded reviewer is the authenticated principal, not trusted from the request body.
claims.supporting: api/app/auth.py (Role, get_principal, require_role); router-level auth on parts/issues/bom/scenarios/analytics/copilot; api/app/routers/issues.py (steward-gated decisions, principal.username recorded); tests/unit/test_authorization.py (7 tests); api/tests/test_api.py::test_authorization_enforcement (401/401/403, analyst read allowed, no transition on denial)
claims.known_limitation: DEMONSTRATION auth: identities come from static demo bearer tokens (overridable via BOMG_DEMO_USERS), not an SSO/OIDC IdP; no session management, token expiry, or rate limiting. The authorization logic (role checks, steward-gated decisions, authenticated-actor recording) is production-shaped; the credential source is not.
